free_text: Inelastic scattering of 14 MeV neutrons by medium
Scatterers were placed 76.1 mm from the tritium target
and were of natural elements in a light aluminium
can, 30 mm diameter, 30 mm thick. Chemical purities
measurement of the energy spectra of inelastically
scattered neutrons at 110 degrees. Flight path 1 m.
Start pulses from alpha detector and stop from
Neutron detector was NE213 liquid scintillator
diameter 127 mm, 50 mm thick, with pulse shape
determined by neutron scattered from hydrogen at
Alpha detector was a 0.05 mm thick plastic
scintillator placed 50 mm from the tritium target.
Level densities are derived from measured inelastic
neutron spectra with a compound nucleus - statistical
model theory. A Fermi gas model, assuming a constant
A high energy limit of 6.5 MeV was set to avoid the
contribution from the tail of elastically scattered
A lower energy limit was set for each element,
somewhat higher than the largest energy of neutrons
emitted in (n,2n) reactions. The effect of (n,pn)
and (n,2n) reactions on isotopes of low abundance
For the inverse reaction cross section three different
calibrated using the cross section of neutron elastic
scattering on carbon at 30 and 40 degrees, with
.Background corrections were made with an empty
Uncertainties are standard deviations from a
least squares fit to the experimental data. For the
differential elastic cross sections the overall
relative uncertainty is estimated to be about 8%
A.P.T. Japan code changed to JPN
date is corrected. REACTION codes changed
SD:Updated to new date formats,lower case.
corrected according to last EXFOR rules and Dict.

integration the measured neutron emission spectra over
energy using the values given for the nuclear
temperature and the level density formalism. The
inelastic neutron spectra are extrapolated to lower
